headline: Police remand procedures questioned after 23 allegations of abuse
context: Twenty-three individuals alleged physical abuse during police remand in 2024, prompting calls for reform of remand facility oversight.

cards.big: 23 remand detainees alleged physical abuse by police in 2024
cards.sub: Medical evidence documented injuries consistent with assault in 15 cases

cards.big: Criminal Procedure Code Section 117 allows 14-day police remand for investigation
cards.sub: No mandatory medical examination required upon entry to remand facilities

cards.big: Istanbul Protocol on torture documentation not adopted in Malaysian medical practice
cards.sub: Without standardised documentation, evidence of abuse is easily lost

cards.big: CCTV coverage in remand facilities covers only 40% of common areas
cards.sub: Blind spots in surveillance allow abuse to occur without recording

cards.big: 23 allegations with medical evidence suggest a pattern, not isolated incidents
IPCC opened investigations into 14 of 23 complaints as of March 2025
